summary: Tested multi-image upload feature for comments. All 11 backend tests passed: image upload endpoint accepts base64 data (raw and with data URI prefix), auto-generates filenames, handles multiple sequential uploads to same order, and comments with [img] tags work correctly.
features_tested: POST /api/orders/{order_id}/images - auto-generated filename when not provided: PASS
POST /api/orders/{order_id}/images - 400 error when image_data missing: PASS
POST /api/orders/{order_id}/images - 404 error for invalid order_id: PASS
Multiple sequential uploads (3 images) to same order: PASS
POST /api/orders/{order_id}/comments - content with single [img] tag: PASS
POST /api/orders/{order_id}/comments - content with multiple [img] tags: PASS
GET /api/orders/{order_id}/comments - returns comments with [img] tags preserved: PASS
GET /api/uploads/{filename} - uploaded files accessible via public endpoint: PASS
Authentication required for image upload (401 without token): PASS

minor.issue: BACKEND_PUBLIC_URL env var is empty, so URLs are returned as relative paths (/api/uploads/...). Frontend handles this correctly but full URLs would be cleaner.

critical_code_review_comments: Backend implementation is correct and handles multi-image upload scenarios well:
- Image upload endpoint (orders.py:319-343) correctly handles base64 decoding with/without data URI prefix
- Files are stored in UPLOADS_DIR with order_id prefix for organization
- Comment endpoint accepts [img]url[/img] tags in content without modification
- GET comments returns content with [img] tags preserved for frontend rendering
- processFiles() (line 124) handles multiple file selection from both file input and drag&drop
- Sequential upload loop (lines 81-89) uploads each image and builds content with [img] tags
- imagePreviews state allows preview grid with individual remove buttons
- isDragging state provides visual feedback during drag&drop
seed_data_creation: None - used existing order from database
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-image upload backend is fully functional. Test file test_iteration44_multi_image_upload.py covers: image upload with various base64 formats, multiple sequential uploads, comments with [img] tags, file accessibility, and auth requirements. Frontend testing (drag&drop, preview grid, multiple file selection) would require browser automation with Google OAuth.

verified_data.uploads_directory: /app/backend/uploads/ - writable and contains uploaded test images
